text.title: tdd-workflow Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for tdd-workflow as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Agents skip the RED step: they write the implementation first, then "tests" that validate what they wrote (rationalization, not verification).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Tests pin internal details and break under any refactor. Force RED by requiring failure output as part of the response.

failure-mode.description: Tests pin internal details (private methods, exact log strings) and break under any refactor.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Overly specific assertions couple tests to implementation. Write tests against public interfaces only.

failure-mode.description: Mock-heavy unit tests pass while integration is broken.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mocks hide real dependency failures. Restrict mocking to clearly external boundaries.

failure-mode.description: Coverage % becomes the goal; agents game it with assertions on `True`.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Coverage % is a false metric without mutation testing. Pair coverage with mutation testing or branch coverage.

failure-mode.description: Refactor step is universally skipped — green tests + ugly code is "done" for an LLM unless the loop forces it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Make refactoring a separate agent invocation with the explicit goal "do not add features".
